text: You chaired the meeting, shared the agenda, raised a point, and took the minutes.
Today, inside that meeting, you give your opinion and defend it with I think and in my view.

Point of view

I think we should extend the deadline.
In my view, the contract is too risky.
I agree, but I have one concern.
From my point of view, the client comes first.
Thanks for meeting. Let's compare our views. In my view, the new timeline is too tight.
I agree with your point of view. I think we should ask for more time.
Good. My main argument is that quality matters more than speed here.
I see your point, but we also need to persuade the client, not pressure them.
True. Can we find common ground on a shorter extension?
Yes. Let's emphasize the plan and present one united stance.
I think we should ask for more time.
In my view, quality matters most.
I agree with your point of view.
I see your point, but I am not sure.
Let's find common ground.

Hi Simone, this is Daniel. I have been thinking about the client's proposal, and I want to share my point of view before our discussion. In my view, the new timeline is too tight. I think we should ask for two more weeks. My main argument is simple: quality matters more than speed on a contract this size. I know the client wants it fast, so we will need to persuade them. I believe we can find common ground if we offer a clear plan. I do not want to convince them with pressure, only with good reasons. Please think about your own stance, and we can compare our views tomorrow. Talk soon.

Hi team, this is Simone with a quick recap of our discussion about the client's proposal. Daniel and I shared our points of view openly. At first we did not agree on the timeline. In my view, the deadline was too tight, and Daniel agreed. Our main argument was that quality matters most on a large contract. We decided to persuade the client with a clear plan instead of pressure. After a short debate, we found common ground: we will ask for ten extra days, not two weeks. I want to emphasize one point. We should present one united stance to the client. If you disagree, please tell me before Friday. Thanks for your great work, and let us move forward together.

Daniel shares his point of view in a voice note before the discussion. Simone reads the discussion brief and prepares her own stance. They meet and compare their opinions about the timeline. After a short debate, they find common ground on a new plan. Simone writes a recap and sends one united stance to the client.
